coverage_summary.notes: Filtered to Materials and Processing Technology with curated, chunk-linked vocabulary terms.

unit_pattern_bank.label: Practical making / prototype outcome
unit_pattern_bank.description: Best for units where students design, make, test, refine, and evaluate a practical materials, textile, food, processing, or biotechnology outcome.
unit_pattern_bank.default_formative_checks: Teacher observation, safety/quality checkpoints, photo evidence with captions, and quick student explanation of what was changed and why.
unit_pattern_bank.default_summative_evidence: Completed outcome, process evidence, testing records, feedback, and final evaluation against selected success criteria.

unit_pattern_bank.label: Material selection and sustainability unit
unit_pattern_bank.description: Best for units focused on material properties, smart/traditional materials, sustainability, user needs, lifecycle thinking, and responsible material choices.
recommended_literacy_strategies: summary table: what I tested, found, changed
unit_pattern_bank.default_formative_checks: Short justification checkpoints using material/property evidence, teacher questioning, and peer critique using sentence stems.
unit_pattern_bank.default_summative_evidence: Material comparison, decision matrix, prototype/trial evidence, lifecycle or sustainability justification, and evaluation against the brief.

unit_pattern_bank.label: Process testing and optimisation unit
unit_pattern_bank.description: Best for units where students test materials, ingredients, or processes, collect data, improve quality, and explain optimisation decisions.
recommended_literacy_strategies: summary table: what I tested, found, changed
unit_pattern_bank.default_formative_checks: Review of test data, process logs, quality checks, and teacher questioning during practical work.
unit_pattern_bank.default_summative_evidence: Testing log, process flow, quality assurance evidence, final outcome, and explanation of optimisation decisions.

unit_pattern_bank.label: Food product development unit
unit_pattern_bank.description: Best for food technology units involving ingredient functionality, food safety, cultural context, sensory testing, nutrition, packaging, and fit-for-purpose food outcomes.
recommended_literacy_strategies: reading a design brief with guiding questions
unit_pattern_bank.default_formative_checks: Food safety checks, sensory testing notes, teacher observation, and student explanation of ingredient or process changes.
unit_pattern_bank.default_summative_evidence: Food outcome, sensory/user testing evidence, recipe/process notes, safety and quality evidence, packaging/cultural/sustainability justification, and evaluation against selected SC.

unit_pattern_bank.label: Biotechnology investigation / outcome unit
unit_pattern_bank.description: Best for biotechnology units involving fermentation, enzymes, microorganisms, contamination control, biological variables, ethics, sustainability, and safety.
recommended_literacy_strategies: summary table: what I tested, found, changed
unit_pattern_bank.default_formative_checks: Safety/contamination checkpoints, process observations, data checks, and short evidence-based explanations.
unit_pattern_bank.default_summative_evidence: Biotechnology process/outcome evidence, observation or data log, safety/contamination control evidence, explanation of variables, and evaluation against selected SC.

lesson_template_bank.label: Launch: context, vocabulary and success criteria
lesson_template_bank.student_doing: Students will unpack the unit context, key vocabulary, learning intentions and success criteria, then complete a short planning checkpoint.
lesson_template_bank.knowledge_building: Introduce the unit context, key vocabulary, learning intentions, and success criteria.
lesson_template_bank.sense_making: Students unpack examples, discuss what success could look like, and identify what evidence they may need to collect.
lesson_template_bank.applying_learning: Students choose or confirm their context and complete a short planning checkpoint.
lesson_template_bank.formative_check: Check that students can explain the task, key vocabulary, and first success criteria.

lesson_template_bank.label: Research: explore examples and context
lesson_template_bank.student_doing: Students will explore examples, contexts, users or stakeholders, then record useful information for later design or process decisions.
lesson_template_bank.knowledge_building: Introduce relevant examples, contexts, users, stakeholders, materials, ingredients, processes, or systems.
lesson_template_bank.sense_making: Students compare examples and identify useful features, requirements, constraints, or opportunities.
lesson_template_bank.applying_learning: Students record findings that can be used to guide their own decisions.
lesson_template_bank.formative_check: Review student notes and ask students to identify one useful piece of evidence for their own outcome.

lesson_template_bank.label: Testing: gather evidence
lesson_template_bank.student_doing: Students will carry out a test, collect evidence, compare results, and explain what the evidence shows.
lesson_template_bank.knowledge_building: Review the test purpose and connect it to the selected success criteria.
lesson_template_bank.sense_making: Students carry out a test, compare results, and record evidence in a table, photo log, or notes.
lesson_template_bank.applying_learning: Students explain what the evidence shows and identify one improvement or next step.
lesson_template_bank.formative_check: Review the test evidence and ask students to explain one result or decision.

lesson_template_bank.label: Decision-making: justify choices
lesson_template_bank.student_doing: Students will use evidence, feedback, performance properties, or user needs to justify a material, ingredient, process, or design decision.
lesson_template_bank.knowledge_building: Review the evidence students have collected and model how to link evidence to a decision.
lesson_template_bank.sense_making: Students compare options and identify strengths, limitations, risks, or trade-offs.
lesson_template_bank.applying_learning: Students write, record, annotate, or orally explain a justified decision.
lesson_template_bank.formative_check: Check that students can link a decision to evidence rather than opinion only.

lesson_template_bank.label: Feedback: refine and improve
lesson_template_bank.student_doing: Students will use feedback or testing evidence to refine their plan, process, prototype or final outcome.
lesson_template_bank.knowledge_building: Review previous evidence and model how to use feedback to improve an outcome.
lesson_template_bank.sense_making: Students receive peer, teacher, stakeholder, or test feedback and decide what to change.
lesson_template_bank.applying_learning: Students refine their plan, process, prototype, or final outcome and record the reason for the change.
lesson_template_bank.formative_check: Check that students can link a change to feedback, testing, or success criteria.

lesson_template_bank.label: Making: apply learning
lesson_template_bank.student_doing: Students will make, construct, process, prototype, or apply learning to their selected outcome.
lesson_template_bank.knowledge_building: Review safety, quality, and success criteria before practical work begins.
lesson_template_bank.sense_making: Students use teacher feedback and evidence to make decisions during practical work.
lesson_template_bank.applying_learning: Students make, construct, process, prototype, or apply learning to their selected outcome.
lesson_template_bank.formative_check: Teacher checks safety, quality, and progress evidence during the practical task.

lesson_template_bank.label: Evaluate: final evidence and next steps
lesson_template_bank.student_doing: Students will organise evidence and complete a final evaluation explaining strengths, improvements and next steps.
lesson_template_bank.knowledge_building: Review the selected learning intentions and success criteria.
lesson_template_bank.sense_making: Students organise evidence and identify how their work meets the selected criteria.
lesson_template_bank.applying_learning: Students complete a final evaluation explaining strengths, improvements, and possible next steps.
lesson_template_bank.formative_check: Check that final evidence includes outcome, process, feedback or testing, and explanation.

principle_explanations: Students work with a real-world context and make decisions that connect learning to practical outcomes.
Students have choice in context, evidence format, or design direction while still working toward shared curriculum goals.
Students use testing, modelling, feedback, or data to refine outcomes.
Students explain decisions using evidence rather than opinion only.
Students connect learning to local needs, community issues, industry practice, or future pathways.
tiriti_or_equity_statements: The unit uses local and relevant contexts and gives students choices in how they show evidence of learning.
Learning is scaffolded through explicit vocabulary, visible success criteria, and equitable access to tools, materials, and feedback.
Students consider responsible decision-making, sustainability, and the impact of outcomes on people, place, and community.
The unit includes culturally responsive examples, including Māori innovation or local contexts where appropriate.
Students consider kaitiakitanga, manaakitanga, or community impact when making design or technology decisions.
The task design supports rangatiratanga by giving students meaningful choices and agency.
barrier_reduction_notes: Barriers are reduced through chunked instructions, worked examples, visual checklists, and regular teacher checkpoints.
Students can choose from different evidence formats and receive feedback before final submission.
Key vocabulary and success criteria are explicitly taught before students apply them independently.
Practical tasks are broken into achievable steps with clear safety, quality, and progress checks.
Teachers check for understanding before independent work begins.
Assessment barriers are reduced by allowing oral, visual, practical, annotated, or digital evidence where appropriate.
formative_checks: Teacher observation and questioning during practical work.
Exit ticket linked to the lesson learning intention.
Peer feedback using selected success criteria.
Teacher conference checkpoint before students move on.
Photo evidence checkpoint with a short caption.
Process log check: what I tested, found, changed.
Safety / quality sign-off before practical work continues.
End-of-week checkpoint against selected LI and SC.
summative_evidence: Final outcome plus evaluation against selected success criteria.
Portfolio of planning, testing, feedback, refinement, and final evaluation.
Prototype or practical outcome with testing records and student explanation.
Testing log with data, observations, and improvement decisions.
Stakeholder feedback and documented response to feedback.
Design brief, specifications, outcome, and evaluation.
Quality assurance checklist and final evidence sample.
Short video demonstration with student explanation.
assessment_notes: Assess evidence of progress, decision-making, practical application, and explanation against selected success criteria.
Give students feedback and time to improve before final evidence is collected.
Use multiple evidence types so students are not assessed only through extended writing.
Make learning intentions and success criteria visible before the assessed task.
Use best-fit judgement across evidence rather than relying on one finished product.

webpage_use_model.dependent_filters: Filter curriculum_chunks by selected learning_area, year_level, and top-level strand before showing strands, LI, SC, evidence, and lesson options.
webpage_use_model.strand_selection: Display top_level_strand_label / strand_label as the teacher-facing strand. Display element_label as the more specific sub-area.
webpage_use_model.minimum_coverage_warning: Warn the teacher if fewer than curriculum_coverage_rules.minimum_strands_per_year strands are selected for the year.
webpage_use_model.assessment_descriptors: Use assessment_descriptor_bank for strand/year-level rubrics and best-fit assessment guidance.

pedagogy_dropdowns.recommendation: Where possible, display these as grouped dropdowns/chips rather than one long list.

progression_pathways.purpose: Use progression_pathway_bank to distribute target levels across a generated weekly sequence so students can build gradually from one assessment level toward another.
progression_pathways.lesson_generation_rule: When generating a sequence, assign each lesson a target level from the selected pathway, then preselect success criteria from that level while still displaying all levels for teacher choice.
progression_pathways.learning_intention_rule: Use level_progression_bank to display a graduated learning-intention focus alongside the curriculum chunk learning intentions.
progression_pathways.manual_override_rule: Teachers may override the target level or select SC from any level in any lesson.

weekly_focus_dropdown.recommendation: Provide a weekly focus dropdown generated from previous teacher selections rather than requiring free text.
weekly_focus_dropdown.teacher_override: Keep the text input so the teacher can edit the selected weekly focus.

assessment_level_label.reason: The level is a planning midpoint or typical class level, not a ceiling. The pathway and selected success criteria can support movement toward Exceeding.

weekly_only_planner.purpose: Use one short weekly plan instead of a plan for every lesson.
weekly_only_planner.reason: Teachers can plan the weekly learning arc quickly while still identifying LI, SC, activities, checks, and reflection.
weekly_only_planner.clickable_first: Most weekly fields should be selectable from JSON-backed dropdowns, with a short editable text box for local adjustment.

weekly_sequence_ordering.recommendation: Weekly plans should be reorderable after generation.
weekly_sequence_ordering.renumbering_rule: After a move/add/remove, renumber weeks but preserve teacher-entered content.

weekly_suggestions.recommendation: Provide a 'Suggest empty boxes' action for each week and for the whole unit.
weekly_suggestions.behaviour: Only fill fields that are empty; do not overwrite teacher-entered text unless explicitly requested.

editable_export.recommendation: The final plan should export to an editable Word-compatible document rather than only PDF/print output.
editable_export.preferred_export: Word-compatible .doc generated from the document preview HTML
editable_export.reason: This keeps the standalone webpage simple while allowing teachers to open and edit the final plan in Microsoft Word or similar editors.
editable_export.note: PDF/print remains useful for sharing a locked copy, but Word export is preferred for editable planning.

word_export_readability.recommendation: Use Word-friendly table layout rather than CSS grid/flex in exported .doc files.
formatting_rules: Use shaded section headers for major titles.
Use table cells with generous padding.
Use actual unordered lists for bullet points rather than manual bullet characters with line breaks.
Separate weekly summary information from the activity table.
Use highlighted level tags for success criteria.
Keep page-break-inside: avoid on each weekly section where possible.

word_export_page_layout.page_margins: Minimal margins: 0.6 cm in the Word-compatible export; 6 mm for browser print.
word_export_page_layout.weekly_page_breaks: Each weekly plan starts on a new page using page-break-before / break-before on the week-block section.
word_export_page_layout.spacing_adjustment: Table and section padding reduced slightly to keep content readable with smaller page margins.

weekly_activity_defaults.behaviour: Pedagogical strategy, Do now, Activity 1, Activity 2, Checking for Understanding / Exit Task, and Reflection / Next Progressions start blank.
weekly_activity_defaults.reason: Teachers should choose or fill these using dropdown suggestions rather than accepting auto-filled content.
weekly_activity_defaults.dropdowns: Dropdowns still provide quick suggestions for each empty field.

plan_json_controls.load_plan_json: Load a previously downloaded plan JSON.
plan_json_controls.save_plan_json: Top-toolbar Save plan JSON button downloads the editable planning data as JSON.
plan_json_controls.browser_save: Save in browser remains available for temporary local storage.

newline_fix.issue: Some text fields displayed literal backslash-n sequences.
newline_fix.fix: Use real newline characters for textarea joins/splits and appended quick statements.

weekly_colour_and_navigation.colour_coding: Each week card has a repeating colour class to make the sequence easier to scan in the HTML planner.
weekly_colour_and_navigation.jump_dropdown: The Weekly plans section includes a Jump to week dropdown populated from the generated weekly sequence.

end_of_unit_marking_scheme.purpose: Generate an editable end-of-unit marking scheme from selected curriculum chunks or selected strands.
end_of_unit_marking_scheme.success_criteria_rule: Include all available success criteria for every selected curriculum chunk at every level.
end_of_unit_marking_scheme.webpage: The marking scheme appears in its own editable section before the document preview.
end_of_unit_marking_scheme.print_and_word_export: The same marking scheme is appended to the end of the document preview, browser print output, and editable Word-compatible export.
end_of_unit_marking_scheme.editing: Teachers can tick, delete, edit, or add notes in the webpage before exporting.

print_layout_fix.issue: The previous wide marking table could run off the page in Word because it had one column for each achievement level.
print_layout_fix.fix: The print/Word document now uses a vertical layout: one curriculum area at a time, with Emerging to Exceeding shown as rows.
print_layout_fix.page_break: The end-of-unit marking scheme starts on a new page and each marking area avoids splitting where possible.

multi_week_blocks.purpose: A single plan block can cover a range such as Week 1-3 when a concept takes multiple weeks.
multi_week_blocks.behaviour: Changing weeks_covered recalculates the start/end week range for following blocks while preserving teacher-entered content.
multi_week_blocks.print_word_export: The document exports one page per block, not one page per individual week.

teacher_friendly_guided_ui.kept_curriculum_json_loading: Curriculum JSON remains loadable so other curriculum areas can be used.
changes: Teacher-facing button wording instead of technical JSON wording where possible.
Planning checklist added to show next steps and readiness.
Full planning options can be hidden for a simpler workflow.
Advanced backup remains available but is labelled less technically.
Destructive/reset actions use clearer confirmations.
Marking scheme editor is collapsible in the webpage while still exporting the full scheme.

single_strand_chunk_plans.single_strand_rule: Each unit of work is planned around one strand only.
single_strand_chunk_plans.planning_block_rule: The sequence is generated from selected curriculum chunks/statements rather than from week numbers.
single_strand_chunk_plans.generated_blocks: One editable planning block is created for each selected curriculum chunk.
single_strand_chunk_plans.teacher_adjustments: Teachers can reorder chunk plans, estimate lesson counts, add custom plans, and fill empty suggestions.

live_marking_scheme_updates.issue_fixed: The end-of-unit marking scheme did not immediately update when strands were changed or deselected.
behaviour: The marking scheme now syncs whenever the marking editor renders.
Rows for deselected curriculum chunks or strands are removed automatically.
Rows for newly selected chunks/strands are added automatically.
Custom teacher-added rows are preserved.
actions_that_sync: Choosing a project context that sets the strand

marking_scheme_selected_sc_only.issue_fixed: The marking scheme generated a generic strand row with placeholder descriptors such as 'Add Developing success criteria for Materials and Processing Technology'.
new_behaviour: The marking scheme is generated only from selected curriculum chunks/statements.
Generic strand-only rows are no longer created.
If a selected chunk has no success criteria, it does not create placeholder marking rows.
Changing strands still clears any selected chunks that no longer match, which also removes their marking rows.

plan_level_curriculum_dropdowns.purpose: Each generated or manually added curriculum chunk plan can still choose/change the curriculum focus, Knowledge, Practices, LI and SC.
plan_level_curriculum_dropdowns.restriction: Dropdown options are limited to the curriculum chunks/statements selected by the teacher in the Curriculum selection section.
plan_level_curriculum_dropdowns.editable_after_selection: After dropdown selection, textareas remain editable for local adaptation.

single_curriculum_file_workflow.purpose: Teachers load only the curriculum file for the strand they are planning.
single_curriculum_file_workflow.simplification: The planner can auto-select the only available strand in this JSON file.

key_vocabulary.trigger: When the teacher selects the literacy strategy 'pre-teach key vocabulary', each curriculum chunk plan displays clickable vocabulary terms.
key_vocabulary.teacher_control: Teachers click vocabulary terms to include them in the chunk plan, and can still edit the textarea.
key_vocabulary.display_behaviour: The Key vocabulary to pre-teach section starts minimised. Teachers can open it to type directly or select vocabulary chips.
key_vocabulary.open_state_behaviour: The vocabulary panel stays open after a teacher selects a vocabulary chip and remembers manual open/close state during editing.
key_vocabulary.print_word_layout: In the print and Word-compatible export, selected key vocabulary is displayed in a two-column table to reduce vertical space.

curation_update.change: Vocabulary chips have been reduced and linked more tightly to Materials and Processing Technology chunks.
curation_update.avoid_overload_rule: Display only curriculum_chunks[].vocabulary_terms for each selected chunk plan.
curation_update.required_terms_rule: Required strand, assessment, and NCEA-style command words remain in vocabulary_bank, while chunk-level displays only show relevant terms.

quick_chunk_focus_updates_vocabulary.change: Quick chunk focus options derived from curriculum chunks carry the source chunk ID.
behaviour: Choosing a curriculum or LI-based chunk focus updates the plan's curriculum_chunk_id.
The vocabulary chips shown in Key vocabulary to pre-teach are filtered to that chunk.
Existing selected vocabulary is kept only if it belongs to the newly selected chunk.

chunk_focus_label.change: UI wording changed from Weekly focus to Chunk focus.
chunk_focus_label.reason: Planning blocks are now based on selected curriculum chunks/statements rather than weeks.

plan_update_checks.issue_fixed: Lesson/chunk plan success criteria did not reliably update when changed.
fixes: Progression level changes now refresh the suggested success criteria for the selected curriculum chunk.
Manual SC textarea editing now parses [Level] Success criterion lines correctly.
The general field update handler now routes progression level changes through a dedicated update function.
Knowledge, Practices, LI, SC, chunk focus, and vocabulary update paths were statically checked.

chunk_level_strategy_selection.change: Whole-unit literacy, numeracy, learner support and extension strategies selected in section 3 now become selectable inside each curriculum chunk plan.
behaviour: Each chunk plan has a collapsed section for strategy selection and explanation.
Only strategies already selected in Whole-unit planning are available at chunk level.
Teachers can choose the strategies relevant to that chunk and explain how they will be used.
Selections and explanations export into the Word document and print view.
If a whole-unit strategy is deselected, it is removed from chunk-level selections on next render.

chunk_pedagogical_strategy.change: Pedagogical strategy in each curriculum chunk plan can now be selected from a dropdown or typed/adapted directly.
behaviour: Dropdown selection fills the editable text box.
Teachers can type their own pedagogical strategy wording.
The chosen/typed strategy exports into the Word document and print view.

additional_chunk_activities.change: Each curriculum chunk plan now starts with one activity only, with the option to add more.
behaviour: Activity 1 is shown by default.
Teachers can click Add activity to create Activity 2, Activity 3, and so on.
Each added activity has an editable title.
Teachers can choose a suggested activity or type/adapt their own.
Added activities can be removed.
Additional activities export into the Word document and print view after Activity 1.
Older saved plans with content in the previous fixed Activity 2 field are migrated into the addable activities list.

chunk_pedagogical_strategies.change: Each curriculum chunk plan can now select more than one pedagogical strategy.
behaviour: Pedagogical strategies display as clickable chips.
Teachers can select multiple strategies.
Teachers can still type or adapt additional pedagogical strategy wording.
Selected strategies and typed/adapted wording export into the Word document and print view.
Older saved plans with a single selected pedagogical strategy are migrated into the multi-select list when possible.

changes: Removed Quick chunk focus and editable Chunk focus from each chunk plan.
Curriculum chunk focus is now the main and quicker way to set the chunk plan focus.
LIT / NUM is derived automatically from selected chunk-level literacy and numeracy strategies.
Key vocabulary appears on chunk plans by default and remains collapsed/openable for typing or selection.
Key vocabulary exports only when vocabulary has been selected or typed.
simplified_chunk_focus_and_lit_num.lit_num_rule: LIT if literacy strategies or vocabulary are selected; NUM if numeracy strategies are selected; LIT + NUM if both apply.

learning_focus_wording_and_reflection.wording_change: Teacher-facing wording now uses 'learning focus' instead of 'chunk' wherever practical.
learning_focus_wording_and_reflection.internal_note: Internal field names may still use chunk for backwards compatibility with saved planning files.

reflection_change.behaviour: Reflection is no longer an editable pre-print planning field.
reflection_change.export: The Word/print document includes guided reflection prompts and blank writing space for the teacher to complete after teaching.
guided_prompts: What needs reteaching or practising again?
What should be adjusted before the next lesson or learning focus?
Which students are ready for extension?
What practical, safety, or resource issues need following up?

removed_redundant_evidence_support_exten.change: Removed the separate Evidence / support / extension textarea from each learning focus plan.
removed_redundant_evidence_support_exten.reason: Support and extension are already handled through selectable strategy buttons and the explanation box inside each learning focus plan.
removed_redundant_evidence_support_exten.export_change: The redundant Evidence / support / extension row is no longer included in the Word/print document.

additional_information_label.change: Renamed 'Explanation for this learning focus' to 'Additional information (if used)'.
additional_information_label.reason: The field is optional and should not imply teachers must write an explanation every time.
additional_information_label.export_change: The Word/print document labels this optional note as Additional information.

changes: Teacher-facing plan wording now uses learning focus instead of chunk wherever practical.
Each learning focus plan is collapsible.
Each learning focus plan has a colour-coded completeness status badge.
The relevant marking guide appears directly after the matching learning focus in the web page and Word/print document.
The final whole-document marking scheme is no longer appended separately in the Word/print output.
The Marking scheme editor remains available for adjusting wording before export.
The page styling was refreshed with cleaner cards, summaries, badges, and status highlights.
completeness_checks: Checking for Understanding / Exit Task filled

visible_chunk_wording_cleanup.change: Removed remaining teacher-facing uses of the word chunk from instructions, checklist labels, empty-state text, alerts, and learning focus plan wording.
visible_chunk_wording_cleanup.note: Internal code identifiers may still use chunk for backwards compatibility, but the visible planning workflow uses learning focus/curriculum statement language.

webpage_use_model.final_visible_wording_patch: Removed the remaining visible instance of 'chunk' from the page description.

changes: Learning focus areas now start collapsed by default.
When a teacher edits the Marking scheme editor, the matching 'Marking guide for this learning focus' updates immediately on the page.
The Word/print document preview also updates immediately from marking scheme edits.

word_export_narrow_margins.change: Word export now uses a named Word page section with narrow margins.
word_export_narrow_margins.margin_setting: 0.5 inch on all sides, matching Word's narrow margin style.
word_export_narrow_margins.extra: Header and footer margins set to 0.25 inch; body margin set to 0.

changes: Word export now uses @page Section1 and wraps the body in div.Section1 so Word applies the margin settings more reliably.
Margins are set to 0.35 inch on all sides to make the narrow margins obvious.
Header and footer margins are set to 0.15 inch.
A Teacher feedback row with blank lined space is added after the reflection prompts for each learning focus.
The feedback space uses a table with blank rows so Word preserves the writing space more reliably than simple div height.

defaults_and_overrides_workflow.purpose: Reduce double handling while keeping teacher customisation.
changes: Learning focus strategies now default from the whole-unit strategy selections using simple curriculum-focus relevance rules.
Teachers can choose Use unit defaults or Customise this focus inside each learning focus.
Key vocabulary is auto-selected from the curriculum focus by default and can still be edited with chips/text.
A Needs attention panel summarises missing core fields so teachers do not need to open every learning focus.
The Word document uses the same generated/defaulted strategy selections unless the teacher customises them.
defaults_and_overrides_workflow.default_rule: Defaults are generated from the curriculum focus and unit-level selections. Custom mode stores teacher-selected overrides.

changes: Curriculum statement cards are colour-coded by sub-area using values read from the loaded curriculum JSON.
Section and sub-area badges are shown on each curriculum statement card.
The search bar has been shortened and paired with a clickable sub-area filter.
Sub-area filter options are generated dynamically from chunk fields such as element_label, subarea_label, sub_area, area_label, or area.
This works with future curriculum JSON files without hard-coding Materials and Processing sub-area names.

student_friendly_li_sc_options.change: Added student-friendly LI and SC wording options, while preserving original teacher/curriculum wording.
student_friendly_li_sc_options.recommended_webpage_behaviour: Use a teacher toggle to generate learning focus LI/SC from student-friendly wording or teacher/curriculum wording.

student_friendly_li_sc_web_support.change: Planner can now generate LI and SC from either teacher/curriculum wording or student-friendly wording.
web_controls: Setup checkbox: Use student-friendly LI / SC wording
LI dropdown labels show Student LI or Teacher LI depending on mode
SC dropdown labels show Student SC or Teacher SC depending on mode
Generated student-friendly success criteria carry Start / Build / Show stage prompts into the Word document
student_friendly_li_sc_web_support.preservation: Original curriculum source statements and teacher SC remain in the JSON and are still available when the toggle is off.

deduplicated_li_stage_display.change: Duplicate LI Start/Build/Show stage sets are grouped in the planner and Word export.
behaviour: The Learning Intentions still display individually.
If several LI share the same staged support, the stages are displayed once.
The grouped stage set says which LI it applies to.
The JSON now includes learning_intention_stage_groups for future interfaces.

unit_patterns_from_json.change: Unit pattern options are strand-specific and stored in this curriculum JSON.
unit_patterns_from_json.webpage_rule: The planner should populate the Unit pattern dropdown from unit_pattern_bank in the loaded JSON, not from hard-coded webpage options.

progress_descriptor_scale.descriptor: The student requires significant support and is beginning to understand foundational ideas for the year level.
The student shows flashes of understanding and is building capability, but learning is inconsistent and still needs support.
The student demonstrates expected skills more often than not and completes tasks with increasing independence.
The student consistently meets curriculum expectations, applies learning confidently across tasks, and is ready for the next year’s learning.
The student works beyond expectations, applying advanced knowledge to complex or unfamiliar situations.

levels.descriptor: With significant support, the student can follow basic safety instructions and complete parts of a materials, food, processing, or biotechnology task. They are beginning to identify materials, ingredients, tools, risks, and simple performance properties, but choices are mostly directed by the teacher.

The student can select and use some materials, ingredients, tools, or joining/processing methods safely when prompted. They attempt testing, modelling, or observations, and can describe some links between properties, processing choices, sustainability, cultural context, and fitness for purpose, but explanations are partial.

The student usually works safely and can select, use, test, and refine materials, ingredients, tools, or processes with increasing independence. They use evidence such as measurements, observations, trials, stakeholder feedback, or sensory testing to explain how their outcome meets the brief.

The student consistently applies Year 9 materials and processing knowledge to create a safe, functional, and fit-for-purpose outcome. They justify material, ingredient, tool, joining, or processing decisions using evidence, performance properties, risk management, sustainability, user needs, and cultural or environmental considerations.

The student independently makes thoughtful, evidence-rich decisions in a more complex materials, food, processing, textile, or biotechnology context. They test and compare options, manage risks, refine quality, reduce waste, and clearly explain trade-offs between performance, sustainability, ethics, culture, and user needs.

source_ref.source_note: Descriptors support best-fit teacher judgements across the selected strand.

levels.descriptor: With significant support, the student can use materials, ingredients, tools, or processing methods in a controlled task and identify some safety or quality requirements. They are beginning to understand codes of practice, quality assurance, advanced joinery, lifecycle planning, industry standards, or biotechnology optimisation.

The student can carry out some testing, construction, food processing, or biotechnology steps safely and can describe how properties or processing conditions affect an outcome. Their use of evidence, regulatory expectations, quality systems, lifecycle thinking, and ethical considerations is developing but inconsistent.

The student usually refines a materials, food, processing, textile, or biotechnology outcome using testing, feedback, quality checks, and performance evidence. They increasingly manage tools/processes independently and can explain links to safety, sustainability, industry practice, cultural appropriateness, and user needs.

The student consistently meets Year 10 expectations by independently designing, making, refining, and evaluating a reliable fit-for-purpose outcome. They justify choices using performance properties, codes of practice, quality assurance, lifecycle planning, scientific principles, sustainability, cultural responsibility, and ethical impacts.

The student works beyond Year 10 expectations by optimising a complex materials, processing, food, textile, wearable technology, or biotechnology outcome. They use systematic testing, modelling, quality assurance, advanced techniques, and ethical/lifecycle analysis to improve performance, safety, sustainability, and user value.

source_ref.source_note: Descriptors support best-fit teacher judgements across the selected strand.

whole_program_reporting_descriptors.descriptor: The student is beginning to engage with Year 9/10 Technology learning. They require significant support to understand briefs, use tools/processes safely, apply subject vocabulary, and explain design or technical decisions.

The student shows developing understanding of Year 9/10 Technology concepts and practices. They can complete parts of tasks and show flashes of capability, but need support to use evidence, refine outcomes, and explain decisions consistently.

The student is working towards secure Year 9/10 Technology achievement. They usually apply knowledge and practices, use subject language, work with increasing independence, and use feedback or testing to improve outcomes.

The student is consistently meeting Year 9/10 Technology expectations. They apply knowledge confidently, make evidence-based decisions, use appropriate tools/processes, create fit-for-purpose outcomes, and evaluate impacts on people, communities, and the environment.

The student is working beyond Year 9/10 Technology expectations. They independently apply advanced knowledge to complex contexts, justify trade-offs, optimise outcomes, and show mature understanding of ethical, cultural, environmental, social, and technical impacts.

literacy.display_note: Use clear strategies that directly support Technology reading, writing, vocabulary, explanation, and evidence.
options: reading a design brief with guiding questions
summary table: what I tested, found, changed

numeracy.display_note: Use numeracy strategies that naturally occur in Technology tasks: measuring, data, testing, cost, risk, and decision-making.

te_tiriti_equity_reducing_barriers.display_note: Use practical, authentic, non-tokenistic strategies that reduce access barriers and support culturally responsive learning.
options: connect to place, community, or local environmental issues
include mana whenua or local iwi context where appropriate and approved
include mātauranga Māori where appropriate, authentic, and not tokenistic
use te reo Māori vocabulary accurately and respectfully
include Māori designers, technologists, innovators, or local examples
consider kaitiakitanga / guardianship in design decisions
consider manaakitanga / care for people in user-centred design
give students meaningful choice in context or evidence format
allow oral, visual, practical, digital, and written evidence options
explicitly teach vocabulary and success criteria
provide equitable access to tools, materials, and feedback
check for bias in examples, data, algorithms, or design assumptions

learner_support.display_note: Use concrete scaffolds that make tasks clearer, safer, and more achievable.

extension.display_note: Use challenge options that increase complexity, independence, optimisation, transfer, or authentic audience.
options: create a second version for a different user group

assessment.display_note: Short list of clear, high-utility assessment options for fast dropdown selection.
formative_checks: Teacher observation and questioning during practical work.
Exit ticket linked to the lesson learning intention.
Peer feedback using selected success criteria.
Teacher conference checkpoint before students move on.
Photo evidence checkpoint with a short caption.
Process log check: what I tested, found, changed.
Safety / quality sign-off before practical work continues.
End-of-week checkpoint against selected LI and SC.
summative_evidence: Final outcome plus evaluation against selected success criteria.
Portfolio of planning, testing, feedback, refinement, and final evaluation.
Prototype or practical outcome with testing records and student explanation.
Testing log with data, observations, and improvement decisions.
Stakeholder feedback and documented response to feedback.
Design brief, specifications, outcome, and evaluation.
Quality assurance checklist and final evidence sample.
Short video demonstration with student explanation.
assessment_notes: Assess evidence of progress, decision-making, practical application, and explanation against selected success criteria.
Give students feedback and time to improve before final evidence is collected.
Use multiple evidence types so students are not assessed only through extended writing.
Make learning intentions and success criteria visible before the assessed task.
Use best-fit judgement across evidence rather than relying on one finished product.

pedagogy_research_sources.title: CAST UDL Guidelines 3.0
used_for: multiple means of engagement, representation, action and expression
choice, relevance, collaboration, feedback, vocabulary/symbol support, accessible materials, and progress monitoring

pedagogy_research_sources.title: EEF Improving Literacy in Secondary Schools
used_for: subject-specific reading, writing, speaking, listening, and vocabulary development

pedagogy_research_sources.title: EEF Improving Mathematics in Key Stages 2 and 3
used_for: teacher judgement, content knowledge, and understanding common difficulties in mathematics learning

pedagogy_research_sources.title: EEF Special Educational Needs in Mainstream Schools
used_for: high-quality teaching for students with additional needs

Emerging.learning_intention_focus: We are learning to identify the key idea, tool, material, system, process, user need, or safety/quality requirement connected to this lesson.
Emerging.teacher_focus: Use modelling, worked examples, vocabulary support, safety checks, and highly scaffolded evidence collection.
typical_student_evidence: labels key parts or vocabulary
follows a worked example
identifies one safety, quality, user, or responsible-practice requirement
collects simple teacher-supported evidence

Developing.learning_intention_focus: We are learning to describe how the selected knowledge or practice affects an outcome and carry out some relevant steps safely.
Developing.teacher_focus: Move from modelling to guided practice; prompt students to describe links between decisions, evidence, and outcomes.
typical_student_evidence: describes a simple cause/effect link
uses a checklist or scaffold to complete practical steps
records early test results or observations
explains one decision with support

Consolidating.learning_intention_focus: We are learning to use evidence, feedback, testing, modelling, observations, or data to improve our work and explain decisions.
Consolidating.teacher_focus: Require students to use evidence and feedback to make changes; strengthen independence and accuracy.
typical_student_evidence: uses testing or feedback to make an improvement
explains links to safety, quality, sustainability, user needs, culture, ethics, data, or reliability
records evidence across more than one lesson
uses relevant vocabulary with increasing accuracy

Proficient.learning_intention_focus: We are learning to independently justify choices and evaluate how well the outcome or process is fit-for-purpose.
Proficient.teacher_focus: Expect independent decision-making, stronger justification, and explicit links to selected success criteria.
typical_student_evidence: justifies decisions using relevant evidence
evaluates against the brief, user needs, or performance requirements
works independently through a sequence of practical/design/digital decisions
uses subject-specific vocabulary accurately

Exceeding.learning_intention_focus: We are learning to optimise or extend our outcome by using stronger evidence, testing, modelling, feedback, ethical reasoning, lifecycle thinking, reliability, or impact analysis.
Exceeding.teacher_focus: Create opportunities for optimisation, transfer to a new constraint/user/context, advanced evidence, and trade-off reasoning.
typical_student_evidence: optimises an outcome or process
tests alternatives or adds a more complex constraint
uses trade-off reasoning across quality, cost, sustainability, ethics, user value, reliability, or impact
works beyond the expected level with independence

progression_pathway_bank.label: Emerging → Exceeding
progression_pathway_bank.description: Use for a full progression where the unit deliberately moves students from access and identification toward optimisation and extension.
progression_pathway_bank.distribution: Spread the levels gradually across the generated weekly sequence.

progression_pathway_bank.label: Emerging → Proficient
progression_pathway_bank.description: Use when the core class target is Year-level proficiency, while still allowing students to select Exceeding SC manually.
progression_pathway_bank.distribution: Spread the levels gradually across the generated weekly sequence.

progression_pathway_bank.label: Developing → Proficient
progression_pathway_bank.description: Use when students already have basic access to the strand and need to build toward independent justification.
progression_pathway_bank.distribution: Spread the levels gradually across the generated weekly sequence.

progression_pathway_bank.label: Developing → Exceeding
progression_pathway_bank.description: Use when students begin with some prior knowledge and the unit has enough challenge for optimisation/extension.
progression_pathway_bank.distribution: Spread the levels gradually across the generated weekly sequence.

progression_pathway_bank.label: Consolidating → Exceeding
progression_pathway_bank.description: Use for a shorter or more advanced unit where the main class target is independent, high-quality evidence and extension.
progression_pathway_bank.distribution: Spread the levels gradually across the generated weekly sequence.

progression_pathway_bank.label: Teacher-selected target level only
progression_pathway_bank.description: Use the whole-unit target level for all generated lessons. Teachers can still select SC from any level manually.
progression_pathway_bank.distribution: No gradual distribution; use the selected target level.

ui_labels.assessment_level_focus_note: This is the typical class level for planning and reporting, not a cap on achievement. Progression pathways can still move students toward higher levels.
ui_labels.weekly_focus_note: Weekly focus options should be generated from prior selections: selected curriculum chunks, strands, project context, learning intentions, evidence, lesson templates, unit pattern, and progression stage.

phs_weekly_plan_template.mana_ako_note: All lessons are underpinned by the MANA AKO learning dispositions framework: learning focused relationships, routines for learning and expectations should be explicitly taught.
phs_weekly_plan_template.rationale: The original PHS template is lesson-based. This planner adapts the same headings into one short weekly plan to reduce teacher workload.

fields.label: Reflection | Next learning progressions / Who needs further support

do_now_options: What changed? review of previous evidence
Student self-check against last success criteria
activity_1_options: Explicit teaching of key concept or process
Demonstration of tool, material, system, process, or code
Plan or update design brief / specifications
Set up testing method or data table
activity_2_options: Peer or stakeholder feedback and response
check_exit_options: Exit ticket against weekly learning intention
Peer feedback using selected success criteria
Process log: what I tested, found, changed
Quick oral explanation of one decision
reflection_next_options: Identify students needing re-teach next week
Adjust next week based on evidence collected
Note resources or safety issue to prepare

planner_note: Single-strand curriculum file. Teachers load one curriculum file for the strand they are working on.

vocabulary_bank.display_policy: Use curriculum_chunks[].vocabulary_terms for the visible chip list. This avoids overloading teachers.
vocabulary_bank.usage_note: Required terms are stored in this bank, but the webpage should show only chunk-level vocabulary_terms by default.

checked_against: Technology Year 9 Teaching Sequence, October 2025, Materials and Processing Technology pages 10-11
Technology Year 10 Teaching Sequence, October 2025, Materials and Processing Technology pages 10-11
curriculum_alignment_review.result: All 52 Materials and Processing Technology curriculum source statements in this JSON matched the uploaded Year 9 and Year 10 teaching-sequence source statements after normalising line breaks and hyphenation.
notes: Inventor/example biography bullets from the source documents are not treated as required curriculum statements.
Learning intentions and success criteria remain derived from the matched curriculum source statements and assessment descriptors.
Student-friendly LI/SC options have been added as alternate display wording; original curriculum-aligned teacher wording remains unchanged.

starting_point_bank.label: Biotechnology
starting_point_bank.description: Selects all 8 curriculum statement(s) in the Biotechnology sub-area and applies the Biotechnology investigation / outcome unit pattern.

starting_point_bank.label: Food technology
starting_point_bank.description: Selects all 13 curriculum statement(s) in the Food technology sub-area and applies the Food product development unit pattern.

starting_point_bank.label: Hard (resistant) and soft (textiles) engineering
starting_point_bank.description: Selects all 12 curriculum statement(s) in the Hard (resistant) and soft (textiles) engineering sub-area and applies the Practical making / prototype outcome pattern.

starting_point_bank.label: Materials engineering
starting_point_bank.description: Selects all 4 curriculum statement(s) in the Materials engineering sub-area and applies the Material selection and sustainability unit pattern.

starting_point_bank.label: Materials technology
starting_point_bank.description: Selects all 4 curriculum statement(s) in the Materials technology sub-area and applies the Material selection and sustainability unit pattern.

starting_point_bank.label: Processing technology
starting_point_bank.description: Selects all 11 curriculum statement(s) in the Processing technology sub-area and applies the Process testing and optimisation unit pattern.

change_log.summary: Starting points now represent curriculum sub-areas and include explicit chunk_ids so choosing one selects all/suitable statements in that sub-area and applies its pattern.
Added barrier-based diverse-needs support bank for unit-level planning and learning-focus accountability. No student names or diagnoses are stored.

Literacy access: labelled diagram instead of full paragraph
Extension / high potential: second iteration for different user group
